changes.description: Tighten AHE evidence-classification guidance so coercive '/feature' flow-test prompts (for example, prompts that explicitly demand talking to every agent/sidecar) are tagged as synthetic routing tests and cannot, by themselves, justify a sidecar-overreach regression claim without a second proof source.
changes.failure_pattern: The evaluator can treat a coercive routing-test tree as if it were a natural '/feature' request, producing a false positive that normal feature flow now overuses evaluator/debugger/evolver.
changes.root_cause: Workflow-level evidence selection/classification error: scenario S3 mixed a synthetic prompt ('Habla con cada agente...') into the negative corpus for normal '/feature' behavior, so the observed sidecar fan-out is non-diagnostic for ordinary routing.
changes.why_this_component: The debugger attributes the problem to benchmark hygiene and evidence attribution, not to '/feature' routing logic or any single agent prompt. Updating the AHE workflow contract is the most local fix that addresses the root cause without re-opening the already rejected mechanism 'mech-sidecars-for-every-feature'.

changes.description: Reframe 'node --test scripts/check-harness.test.mjs' in AHE docs as a long validation with an explicit timeout budget and runtime recording requirement, separate from the quick smoke check 'node scripts/check-harness.mjs', so timeout cancellations are not misreported as functional harness failures.
changes.failure_pattern: A passing checker suite can be marked as a baseline failure when run under the default 120s command timeout, because external cancellation arrives near the suite's runtime ceiling.
changes.root_cause: Operational framing/budget issue: the full checker suite passed 14/14 in about 113s when given enough time, but the evaluator treated a 120s timeout cancellation as a functional failure even though the suite cost is dominated by copying the full repo per test.
changes.why_this_component: The supported root cause is not a proven logic defect in scripts/check-harness.test.mjs but how AHE validation budgets and expectations are documented and applied. A workflow-level change is smaller and more evidence-backed than changing test code or checker internals prematurely.
